title: Sebi moves to curb misuse of exchange data with new 30-day rule
summary: Sebi has announced that market price data for listed companies will be available with a 30-day lag for investor education and awareness activities. This new regulation, effective July 1, 2026, aims to prevent data misuse and ensure educational content remains current. The National Institute of Securities Markets has received a special exemption.

title: Citi downgrades India to 'Underweight' amid earnings and geopolitical risks
summary: Mumbai Citi has downgraded India to underweight, citing persistent macroeconomic and geopolitical challenges alongside weaker earnings projections. The brokerage anticipates an 11.7% upside for the Nifty, reaching 27,000.  While acknowledging lighter market positioning, Citi's earnings forecasts for FY27-28 are still factoring in the Iran War's impact.  Banks, telecom, defence, and pharma are favored sectors.

title: Weak margins, treasury income drag SBI shares lower
summary: State Bank of India shares experienced their largest single-day drop in two years, falling nearly 7% after disappointing fourth-quarter earnings. The decline was attributed to reduced net interest margins and lower treasury income. Analysts suggest potential further dips, with support expected around the ₹970-990 zone.

title: Geopolitical tensions drag markets lower, but Nifty ends week in green
summary: Indian equities closed lower Friday, impacted by renewed US-Iran tensions, though the market secured weekly gains thanks to falling oil prices. The Nifty 50 and Sensex saw dips, with investor sentiment cautious due to geopolitical risks and potential impacts on corporate earnings from rising input costs. Sectoral performance was mixed, with banking stocks facing pressure.

title: To exercise ESOPs, staff of listed cos can pledge shares in trading window closures
summary: Employees exercising stock options can now pledge shares for funds during trading window closures, thanks to a Sebi clarification. This provides regulatory comfort for bona fide transactions, aiding employees who rely on financing to exercise vested options. However, lenders invoking pledged shares will still face contra-trade restrictions.

title: Stock picks of the week: 5 stocks with consistent score improvement and upside potential of up to 27%
summary: In the coming quarters, two things will impact earnings: High crude oil prices and a weak rupee. A rising dollar puts pressure on the margins of all companies that import raw materials. When margins come under pressure, they lose the ability to think about, or plan for, expansions. That will show up as an impact later. However, it is not the first time (nor will it be the last) that this is happening. Our selected stocks for today depict a strong upward trajectory in their overall average score which is based on five key pillars: Earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk, and price momentum. This implies that there has been a significant improvement in their market outlook in the given time frame.

title: RBI imposes penalties on YES Bank, Hinduja Housing Finance
summary: The RBI said the bank failed to put in place a system of using KYC Identifier assigned by Central KYC Records Registry for the purpose of establishing an account-based relationship with customers.

title: Three pharma stocks where FIIs raised stake in the March quarter
summary: Pharma stocks have been attracting FIIs. Here are three stocks where FIIs have hiked stake in the March 2026 quarter.

title: Accor raises its India play as domestic luxury demand reshapes hotels
summary: Accor India's chairman Gaurav Bhushan said that the luxury hotel sector has significant growth potential. With a focus on domestic travellers, Accor plans to expand its portfolio to 300 hotels by 2030, capitalizing on the increasing demand for high-quality hospitality experiences.

title: BlackRock bets big on stablecoins with two new crypto-linked money-market funds | Key details explained
summary: BlackRock Inc. plans to launch two money-market funds for stablecoin investors, including a digital version of its $6.1 billion liquidity fund and a new tokenized fund for crypto wallet users.&nbsp;

title: Looking for tax benefit for your car loan? Here's when and how you can get tax deduction for your new vehicle purchase
summary: Buying a car is a significant milestone for many Indians. Tax benefits for home and car loans differ, with deductions for the latter only available under certain circumstances. Here's a look at how it works…

title: Bank holiday today, 9 May? Check if SBI, HDFC, PNB, other banks are open or closed on Saturday with RBI's calendar
summary: Account holders and registered bank customers are eager to know whether 9 May is a bank holiday. Check if SBI, HDFC, PNB and other banks are open or closed on Saturday with RBI's holiday calendar.

title: Tax-saving FDs explained: Lock-in period, tax benefits and banks offering highest interest rates
summary: Fixed deposits remain popular among Indians for their safety and reliable returns. With tax-saving FDs offering deductions up to  ₹1.5 lakh and interest rates as high as 7.90%, they are ideal for risk-averse investors seeking stable wealth accumulation.

title: I asked ChatGPT how to fund a Europe trip—SIPs or credit cards? – its answer was practical
summary: To fund a Europe trip, SIPs are safer and predictable, requiring monthly savings of  ₹24,000- ₹25,000. Credit cards can be cost-efficient if repayments align with bonuses, but delays can lead to high costs. A hybrid strategy is recommended for cost efficiency.

title: RBI moots safeguards to curb digital payment fraud: How will they protect bank customers?
summary: To curb digital payment fraud, the RBI has suggested measures such as lagged credit, a trusted person authenticating transactions for vulnerable individuals, prescribing a ceiling for annual aggregate credits in a bank account, and providing customer-induced controls, including a kill switch.

title: Vijay Kedia Portfolio Check: 10 stocks surge up to 44% — plus 5 fresh picks
summary: In terms of CY26 price performance, the portfolio delivered mixed results, with 13 stocks posting positive returns, while the remaining holdings registered losses. ETMarkets has highlighted 10 stocks that gained between 10% and 44%. The analysis also points to five new additions made during the March 2026 quarter. (Data Source: ACE Equity, Trendlyne)

title: Concurrent Gainers: 14 stocks gain for 5 straight sessions, rally up to 25%
summary: Among these, 14 stocks posted gains in all five sessions, delivering cumulative returns of up to 25% during the period. (Data Source: ACE Equity)

title: FII ownership hits 14-year low to 14.7%; DII cushions Indian markets with 18.9% rise: Report
summary: Foreign Institutional Investor (FII) ownership as a percentage of total Indian equities has fallen from 19.9 per cent in April 2016 to 14.7 per cent in April 2026, marking its lowest level since June 2012, according to JM Financial's Fundamental Research report.

title: RBI's ECL framework: HDFC Bank to Axis Bank — experts bet high on these five banking stocks
summary: The RBI's Expected Credit Loss framework, effective April 2027, mandates earlier recognition of loan losses, potentially differentiating stronger banks from weaker ones. While it may pressure PSU banks initially, it aims to enhance transparency and investor confidence in the long run.

title: Stocks to buy under  ₹200: Mehul Kothari of Anand Rathi recommends three shares to buy or sell
summary: BSE Sensex and NSE Nifty ended lower on May 8 but closed the week with gains. The Nifty rose 1%, while broader markets outperformed, with Midcap and Smallcap indices up 4%. Market volatility persisted amid global tensions and profit booking.

title: Buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends three stocks to buy on Monday - 11 May 2026
summary: Sumeet Bagadia from Choice Broking predicts bearish trends for Nifty and Bank Nifty, with support levels at 24,000-24,080 and 54,800-55,000, respectively. He recommends stocks Adani Ports, Torrent Pharma, and GMR Airports for buying.

title: Jupiter Wagons eyes global expansion with BESS, wagon exports
summary: Jupiter Wagons aims to double revenues by 2028-29, also tapping into new mobility solutions and international partnerships.

title: TDS on FD interest decoded: Who pays, how much banks deduct, and exemption rules
summary: Fixed deposit interest provides passive income for many Indians but is subject to TDS under the Income Tax Act. TDS is deducted at 10% if interest exceeds  ₹50,000, with senior citizens enjoying a higher exemption. Forms 15G and 15H can help avoid TDS.

title: How multiple credit card applications influence your credit report and loan approval chances
summary: Multiple credit card applications can lower your credit score due to repeated hard inquiries, which increase lenders' risk perception, while spaced applications and responsible credit usage help maintain a healthy credit profile.

title: Estate planning: Financial mistakes families make after a loved one’s death — and how to avoid them
summary: The death of a family member disrupts finances, making it crucial to focus on financial matters. Key steps include obtaining a death certificate, listing assets and liabilities, managing insurance claims, and re-planning finances to ensure stability during this difficult time.

title: Zerodha’s Nithin Kamath flags ULIP, endowment traps; says health policies remain complex
summary: Nithin Kamath highlights persistent personal finance blunders by Indians, particularly the continued uptake of ULIPs and endowment plans despite ample information. He notes a lack of innovation in these financial missteps, where insurance and investments are wrongly combined. While health insurance faces complexities, Kamath argues these bundled products are easier to scrutinize, making poor choices harder to excuse.

title: 2,00,000% rally! 10 penny stocks that graduated into mid and smallcaps multibaggers
summary: Penny stocks are often seen as the riskiest corner of the market, associated with low liquidity, sharp volatility and speculative trading.

title: Explained! Sebi proposes sweeping changes to buyback framework. 7 things to know
summary: India's securities regulator, SEBI, is proposing major shifts in company share buybacks. Open market buybacks are set to return, and the mandatory involvement of merchant bankers may be reduced. Safeguards for promoter shareholding and minimum public shareholding are also being tightened. These changes aim to simplify business operations while enhancing investor protection.

title: Sebi sets 30-day delay for use of stock price data in educational content; effective from July 1
summary: Sebi has introduced a new rule for market data. Stock exchanges and other institutions must now delay educational price data by 30 days. This change aims to prevent misuse and confusion. The new rule starts from July 1, 2026. NISM, Sebi's training arm, gets an exception for its simulation lab. This move clarifies the difference between education and advice.

title: SBI vs PNB vs BoB: Which PSU bank stock to buy after Q4 results 2026?
summary: PSU banking stocks gained attention post Q4 FY26 earnings, with State Bank of India, Punjab National Bank, and Bank of Baroda showing profit growth. Investors are now prioritizing asset quality and margin trends over earnings figures, leading to varied market reactions.

title: Considering systematic withdrawal plan for retirement? Here's how SWP works, how it is taxed and its benefits, explained
summary: A systematic withdrawal plan generates monthly or quarterly income for investors while keeping the remainder of their mutual fund corpus invested. Here's all you need to know about SWP…

title: How expensive is Gurgaon really? Woman breaks down the real cost of living as a couple in the city
summary: A Gurgaon-based woman has gone viral after sharing a detailed breakdown of her and her husband’s monthly household expenses, revealing that the couple spends around  ₹75,000 to  ₹80,000 every month.&nbsp;

title: 7 hidden credit behaviours that may be hurting your credit score
summary: Protect your credit score from silent damage caused by hidden credit behaviours that reduce creditworthiness, from high utilisation to missed payments and credit report errors.

title: Zerodha's Nithin Kamath warns investors against ULIPs, endowment traps; says people keep making the same money mistakes
summary: Nithin Kamath critiques the continued popularity of ULIPs and endowment plans among Indian investors, despite expert advice against them. He stresses the importance of financial literacy and cautions that access to information alone is insufficient.

title: Meet Tamil Nadu MLA who is 9 times richer than Vijay – inside her  ₹5,863 cr net worth, cars, diamonds and more
summary: Leema Rose, wife of lottery baron Santiago Martin, has declared a staggering net worth of  ₹5,863 crore in her nomination affidavit filed with the Election Commission of India

title: ‘Mumbai takes it back quietly’: Investment banker’s  ₹1.3 lakh monthly expense breakdown goes viral
summary: From  ₹60,000 rent in Worli to  ₹10,000 on Uber rides just to save time—Mumbai-based investment banker Yash Verma breaks down his  ₹1.3 lakh monthly burn rate, proving that in India’s financial capital, time is the ultimate luxury.

title: Estate Planning: Do children have rights over father’s property? Not always — Here’s what the law says
summary: In India, children's legal claims on property depend on whether it is ancestral or self-acquired. Ancestral property grants rights by birth, while self-acquired assets allow fathers complete control. Here are details on whether you can contest it, among others.

title: FIIs sell over Rs 2 lakh crore worth of Indian equities in 2026. What lies ahead?
summary: Foreign investors continue to sell Indian shares in 2026. They have offloaded equities worth over Rs 2 lakh crore this year. This trend marks their third consecutive month as net sellers. Domestic investors are buying, but markets are falling. Experts suggest India is not attracting enough foreign capital. This impacts large companies, while smaller ones get support from local funds.

title: Dividend bonanza on Dalal Street! From MRF to SBI, these large-cap companies announce hefty payouts for FY26
summary: Strong earnings and cash flows have led Indian companies to announce significant dividends for FY26, reflecting confidence in their performance. Notable payers include MRF Limited, Britannia, and SBI, appealing to investors seeking passive income amid market volatility.

title: Planning FD? Here's how much you should invest to earn  ₹10,000 monthly interest — see calculation
summary: Fixed deposits are popular for stable income, particularly among retirees. Banks provide various payout options, and non-cumulative FDs offer periodic interest payments. Here's how much investment is needed to earn  ₹10,000 monthly income.

title: SCSS account rules: Who can invest, how much interest you get and what tax applies
summary: The Senior Citizens Savings Scheme (SCSS) offers Indian retirees a secure investment with guaranteed returns. Eligible individuals can invest a minimum of  ₹1,000, with a maximum limit of  ₹30 lakh. The scheme provides attractive quarterly interest payouts and tax benefits under Section 80C.

title: Ignore market noise, India’s long-term story intact, say D-Street bulls Ramesh Damani and Sunil Singhania
summary: Indian markets face temporary challenges with foreign outflows and geopolitical concerns. However, top investors Ramesh Damani and Sunil Singhania emphasize that India's fundamental growth drivers are strong. They advise retail investors to ignore short-term noise and focus on long-term wealth creation through disciplined investing and compounding. Sectors like defence, infrastructure, and energy offer opportunities.

title: I asked ChatGPT: Which city should I retire to in 2040? How much should I save? AI details how much corpus is needed
summary: ChatGPT suggests a 3.25% withdrawal rate for early retirement in India, requiring an investment of  ₹10-17 crore, depending on the city. It recommends maximising earnings before transitioning to lower-cost cities.

title: Senior citizen FD vs SCSS: Which gives better monthly income after retirement?
summary: Senior citizens can choose between FDs and SCSS for income post-retirement. SCSS often yields higher returns, whereas FDs offer flexibility in contributions and payout structures. Full comparison here.&nbsp;
